BMW Density Design System — Oracle APEX Setup Guide

Apply the BMW Density design system to any Oracle APEX application using workspace-level static files, no server changes required.

APEX 24.x · 26.x Universal Theme 42 Density v11+

Contents

  1. Overview & Files
  2. Step 1 — Sign in to APEX
  3. Step 2 — Navigate to Static Workspace Files
  4. Step 3 — Upload the CSS files
  5. Step 4 — Upload the JS file
  6. Step 5 — Verify the file list
  7. Step 6 — Edit Application Definition
  8. Step 7 — User Interface: add CSS references
  9. Step 8 — User Interface: add JS reference
  10. Step 9 — Set Vita theme style
  11. Step 10 — Global Page: prevent dark-mode FOUC
  12. Step 11 — Verify: light mode & dark mode
  13. Applying to additional apps / workspaces
  14. Updating the files (cache busting)
  15. Troubleshooting

Overview & Files

The BMW Density APEX theme is delivered as four static files. Upload them once to a workspace, then reference them in each application you want to style.

FileDirectoryPurpose
density-apex-theme-standalone.csscss/All --ds-* Density tokens in :root {} + APEX --ut-* variable bridge. Required in every app.
density-apex-overrides.csscss/!important overrides for APEX component classes (Interactive Report, Interactive Grid, Cards, Maps, buttons, dialogs, etc.).
density-dark-mode.csscss/Dark token overrides scoped under body.ds-dark. All the same components, dark palette.
density-dark-mode.jsjs/Dark mode toggle button injected into the APEX user menu. Persists preference in localStorage.
ℹ️
Workspace vs. App files These files go in Workspace Static Files (shared across all apps in the workspace) — not Application Static Files (per-app). The APEX substitution string is #WORKSPACE_FILES#.
1

Sign in to APEX

Navigate to your APEX instance and sign in with your workspace credentials. The workspace must be the one where the target apps live.

ℹ️
BMW internal instance The APEX test instance is at https://apextw10.bmwgroup.net/ords/f?p=4550. Use your BMW network credentials.
2

Navigate to Static Workspace Files

Static Workspace Files are accessible from the Shared Components page of any app in the workspace — or directly via App Builder → Shared Components → Static Workspace Files.

From any app's page list, click Shared Components in the toolbar, then scroll down to the Files section and click Static Workspace Files.

3

Upload the CSS files

Click Create File. The File Editor page opens. Fill in three fields:

  • Directory: css/
  • File Name: the filename (e.g. density-apex-theme-standalone.css)
  • Content: click Choose File and select the CSS file from your machine

Then click Create. Repeat for all three CSS files.

✅
Upload all three CSS files density-apex-theme-standalone.css, density-apex-overrides.css, and density-dark-mode.css — all go into the css/ directory.
4

Upload the JS file

Click Create File again. This time set:

  • Directory: js/
  • File Name: density-dark-mode.js
  • Content: select the JS file
⚠️
Different directory The JS file uses js/ as the directory, not css/. The CSS reference in User Interface Attributes still uses css/ for the CSS files.
5

Verify the file list

Navigate back to Static Workspace Files. You should see all four files listed. The Name column shows the full path including directory.

ℹ️
File URL pattern Once uploaded, the files are accessible at: #WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-apex-theme-standalone.css
This substitution string resolves to the workspace's static file base URL automatically.
6

Edit Application Definition

Go to the App Builder home for the specific application you want to style. Click Edit Application Definition.

The Edit Application Definition page has several tabs at the top. You need the User Interface tab.

7

User Interface — add CSS file references

On the User Interface tab, scroll down to the CSS section. In the File URLs textarea, add the three CSS file paths — one per line:

#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-apex-theme-standalone.css
#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-apex-overrides.css
#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-dark-mode.css
⚠️
Order matters The standalone (tokens) file must come first, overrides second, dark mode third. This ensures the cascade is correct.
ℹ️
Existing entries If the field already contains #IMAGE_PREFIX#pkgapp_ui/css/5.0#MIN#.css, keep that line and add the Density lines below it.
8

User Interface — add JS file reference

On the same User Interface page, scroll down to the JavaScript section. In the File URLs textarea, add:

#WORKSPACE_FILES#js/density-dark-mode.js

Click Apply Changes to save.

✅
What the JS does density-dark-mode.js injects a "Dark Mode" toggle button into the APEX user menu (top-right account icon). It saves the user's preference in localStorage so it persists across sessions.
9

Set Vita theme style

The Density CSS is calibrated against the APEX Vita theme style. Go to Shared Components → Themes, click Universal Theme - 42, then scroll to the Styles section.

Click Vita to open the style detail page, then click Make Current Style (or verify it already shows as current).

ℹ️
Why Vita? The Density token bridge overrides APEX's --ut-* CSS variables. Vita's resolved values are the baseline our overrides were tested against. Using Redwood or other styles is supported but may require additional fine-tuning.

Click the user menu icon (top-right) and select Dark Mode to switch to the dark theme.

10

Global Page — prevent dark-mode FOUC

To avoid a flash of unstyled content (FOUC) when dark mode is enabled, register a small inline script on the application Global Page that applies the saved preference before APEX renders the layout.

10.1 Add Before Header dynamic action (Global Page)

  1. In App Builder, open page 0 Global Page (or create it if your app does not yet use a Global Page).
  2. Add a new Dynamic Action with:
    Event = Page Load
    Selection Type = JavaScript Expression
    JavaScript Expression = window
  3. Add a True Action of type Execute JavaScript Code and paste the following snippet into the Code field:
try {
  var pref = window.localStorage && window.localStorage.getItem('ds-dark-mode');
  if (pref === 'on' && document && document.body) {
    document.body.classList.add('ds-dark');
  }
} catch (e) {
  /* Fail silently — app still renders in light mode */
}
ℹ️
Placement The dynamic action must live on the Global Page (0) so it runs on every page. The Page Load event executes before regions render, which minimizes any light-mode flash when dark mode was previously enabled.
⚠️
Do not duplicate the snippet in static files Keep this FOUC-prevention logic in the Global Page only. The main density-dark-mode.js still owns the toggle behaviour and preference updates; the Global Page snippet only re-applies the preference early.
11

Verify: light mode & dark mode

Run the application. It should now display with BMW Density colours, typography, and spacing in light mode, and switch cleanly to dark mode without a flash of light styling when refreshed in dark mode.

✅
Setup complete! The dark mode preference is saved in localStorage under the key ds-dark-mode. It persists across page navigations and browser restarts.

Applying to additional apps in the same workspace

The static files are uploaded once per workspace. For each additional application in the same workspace, only Steps 7–10 need to be repeated:

  1. Open the app's Edit Application Definition
  2. Go to the User Interface tab
  3. Add the three CSS paths to CSS → File URLs
  4. Add the JS path to JavaScript → File URLs
  5. Set the theme style to Vita

Applying to a different workspace

Upload all four files to the new workspace's Static Workspace Files (Steps 2–6), then follow Steps 7–10 for each app.

Updating the files (cache busting)

When a new version of the Density CSS is available, browsers may serve the old cached version. Force a cache refresh by appending a version query string to the file URLs in the User Interface tab:

#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-apex-theme-standalone.css?v=2
#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-apex-overrides.css?v=2
#WORKSPACE_FILES#css/density-dark-mode.css?v=2

Increment v=N each time you upload a new version of the file. The file name on the server stays the same — you overwrite it via the File Editor's upload form.

⚠️
Update all apps The version query string change must be applied to every app's User Interface → CSS File URLs — it is not workspace-level.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely CauseFix
Page looks unchanged / no Density colours CSS files not referenced correctly, or wrong workspace Open browser DevTools → Network tab. Check the three CSS files load with HTTP 200. Verify the #WORKSPACE_FILES# paths in User Interface Attributes.
CSS loads but colours are wrong Cache — browser serving old CSS Increment ?v=N in the file URL references and hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R).
Dark mode toggle not showing in user menu JS file not referenced; or APEX Universal Theme version changed the nav bar attribute from data-menu-id to data-menu (verified in v87+) Step 1: Check the JS file reference in User Interface → JavaScript → File URLs. Open DevTools console for JS errors.

Step 2: Open DevTools → Console and run: document.querySelectorAll('.t-Header [data-menu]') — should return 2 buttons (About + user menu). If it returns 0, the nav bar structure changed again and the selector in density-dark-mode.js → scanForMenuButtons() needs updating.

Note (v87+): APEX v87 removed the data-menu-id attribute and uses only data-menu. The current JS handles both. If upgrading APEX, re-run the menu DOM diagnostic after each major version bump.
Dark mode toggle shows but switching has no effect density-dark-mode.css missing or not loaded Verify the dark mode CSS is in the File URLs list and loads with HTTP 200.
Charts (OJet DVT) not using BMW colours JET version mismatch (APEX 26+ may use different variable names) Open console and run getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--oj-palette-dvt-rgb-1'). If empty, the variable name changed in the installed JET version — contact the Density team.
Developer toolbar looks wrong in dark mode Density dark mode overriding APEX toolbar styles The guard rules in density-dark-mode.css Section 99 should prevent this. Verify the file is the latest version.
Map controls appear with wrong background MapLibre version upgrade changed class names Check which MapLibre version APEX is using. Our CSS targets .maplibregl-* which is stable across 4.x and 5.x.

Known Issues & APEX Configuration Fixes

P2 — Feedback Nav Link Opens Unbranded APEX Workspace Login

Symptom: The "Feedback" link in the header nav bar redirects users to a raw Oracle APEX workspace login page (no Density styling, no BMW SSO) instead of opening a feedback dialog inside the app.

Root cause: The Feedback nav bar entry is configured as a modal dialog (#action$a-dialog-open?url=...&tmpl=MODAL_DIALOG) pointing to page 10040 of the same application. When the target page requires a separate workspace session that isn't active, APEX falls through to the internal workspace login screen instead of launching the dialog.

Fix options (pick one):

  1. Preferred — Use BMW SSO for the Feedback app too: Ensure the Feedback APEX page (page 10040) uses the same BMW SSO authentication scheme as the rest of the app. In APEX Builder → Shared Components → Authentication Schemes, apply the same OpenID Connect / SAML scheme to page 10040. Then also apply the three Density CSS/JS file references to page 10040's User Interface Attributes so the modal inherits the correct styling.
  2. Quick fix — Remove the Feedback nav entry: If the feedback page is not ready for production, go to APEX Builder → Shared Components → Navigation Bar List → find the "Feedback" entry and delete or hide it until the auth is configured.
  3. Replace with a simple mailto link: Change the Feedback nav entry to mailto:your-team@bmwgroup.com?subject=Supplier+Contact+DB+Feedback as a temporary workaround that always works regardless of session state.

P5 — Home Page Has No Navigation Content

Observation: The app home page (page 1) currently displays only the header bar and app title — the content area is completely empty and the left navigation is hidden (t-PageBody--hideLeft). This means:

  • Users land on a blank page with no indication of where to go next.
  • The Density audit cannot reach any Interactive Report pages because there are no nav links to follow.
  • The IR-specific Density checks (table bg, toolbar colours, IR action buttons) cannot be validated.

Recommended actions:

  1. Add a Left Nav / TreeNav: In APEX Builder → Page 1 → Page Designer, change the Navigation Menu to show a Tree Nav. Add entries for each functional page (e.g. Supplier List, Contact Search, Administration). This also enables the left nav Density overrides already in density-apex-overrides.css (TreeNav bg, hover, active states).
  2. Add a Cards region on the home page: A simple cards region with links to key pages gives users a visual entry point and demonstrates the Density .t-Card overrides (BMW blue icon circles, card borders, hover shadows).
  3. Re-run the Density audit after adding content: Once an IR page is reachable, run audit #6 to validate IR-specific colour overrides (.a-IRR-table, toolbar, sort widget, pagination).

Changelog

DateVersionChanges
2026-06-30 density-dark-mode.js v2 P1 — Dark mode toggle fix for APEX v87+: Rewrote watchForMenu() / scanForMenuButtons() to use a persistent MutationObserver from page load instead of querying for the menu button at DOMContentLoaded (which fails in v87 because APEX hydrates nav bar buttons dynamically after DOMContentLoaded). Added hookedMenuIds guard to prevent double-binding. Added apex.jQuery polling fallback.

P3 — ARIA label injection: Added patchA11y() function that injects aria-label on APEX-generated header elements that lack accessible labels: hamburger/nav-control button, logo link, Install App, Feedback, About, user menu button (labelled "User menu: <username>"), back-to-top button, skip-to-content link, and Oracle APEX footer link.

Dark mode switch visual: Changed toggle switch from <span role="menuitem"> to <button> for correct semantics; added role="switch" + aria-checked on the switch indicator; added separator using native APEX a-Menu-itemSep class so it inherits dark mode styling correctly.
2026-06-29 density-dark-mode.js v1 / CSS v1 Initial deployment. All three CSS files + JS uploaded to workspace static files at v83. Dark mode toggle working in APEX v83.
BMW Group — MN Digital Hub — Updated 2026-06-30
